The Brand

Made in the Skies.
Born in Croatia.

Old Pilot's is a premium Croatian craft spirit from Distillery Duh u Boci in Zagreb. Founded by two ex-pilots who merged their love of flying with an obsession for exceptional spirits. Award-winning across every SKU. Entering 2026 with 13 live UK accounts and 20+ warm pipeline venues.

The Proof

Reorders where
activation happened.

Home House London and Amici Soho have both reordered. Soho House approved at venue level. 80+ buyer touchpoints completed in 2025. Consistently described as one of the best gins tasted by London's most demanding bartenders and buyers. The product works — it just needs activation assets.

The Opportunity

NEBO is coming.
Croatia's first whisky.

The same distillery. The same story. A genuinely rare UK first-mover opportunity in Croatian whisky. Four expressions ready. The Old Pilot's buyer network is the launchpad. The CNTO Peka Party on June 9 is the debut moment. The pipeline is warm. The brand is ready.

The UK Journey

From first import to 2027 vision

Jul 2024
UK Launch
Office established. Cavendish Square, London.
Jul 2025

75 cases / 450 bottles into Grayson's bond.
2025

Hotels, clubs, retail. First reorders at Home House & Amici.
Jun 2026
CNTO Peka Party
~70 guests. Media, trade, NEBO debut. June 9.
H2 2026
Pipeline → 35+
Activation assets. BCB London. WTM. E-commerce live.
End 2027
40+ Accounts
OP + NEBO national. Retail. Self-sustaining UK P&L.

The Full Range

Old Pilot's — Crafted in Croatia.
Made for the World.

Every bottle is distilled, matured and bottled at Distillery Duh u Boci in Zagreb using hand-picked Croatian and Mediterranean botanicals, in small batches. Dreamt up in the clouds and crafted with soul — the passion of two ex-pilots who merged their love of flying with an obsession for exceptional spirits.

Distillery Duh u Boci — Zagreb, Croatia

Every step by hand. Gluten-free. Vegan. Non-GMO.

ABV 45% · 700ml · London Dry

London Dry Gin

"Best London Dry Gin in the World" — International Wine & Spirit Competition (IWSC). The flagship expression of Old Pilot's, recognised on the global stage for exceptional quality and Croatian craft.

Distilled with hand-picked botanicals from the Mediterranean and continental Croatia: juniper, sweet and bitter oranges, lavender, olive leaves, sage and angelica. Each bottle is hand-filled, stamped and sealed with premium wax.

Awards
London Dry Gin Trophy — IWSC (Best London Dry Gin in the World)
Gold Medal — San Francisco World Spirits Competition 2019 & 2021
Gold Medal — World Gin Awards 2022
🌿 Botanicals: Juniper · Sweet & Bitter Orange · Lavender · Olive Leaves · Sage · Angelica
View on oldpilots.com →
ABV 45.5% · 700ml · Aged 6 months · Small Batch 330 btl

Barrel Aged Gin

An age-old tale of exquisite taste. Perfected in first-fill Croatian and American oak barrels using six botanicals from the Mediterranean and continental Croatia — creating a rich, complex flavour that turns every cocktail into an unforgettable experience.

American oak brings sweetness, vanilla and a smoky finish; Croatian oak adds pleasant bitterness and dry fruit. On the nose: sweet juniper, oranges and ripe apricots. On the palate: subtle juniper, round vanilla, dry fruit, long spicy finish with a hint of smoke. Every step — preparation, distillation, maturation, bottling, labelling and wax sealing — done by hand. For every barrel used, a new oak tree is planted.

Awards
Gold Medal — World Gin Awards 2023 & 2022 (Best Croatian Matured Gin)
Gold Medal — The Spirit Business, The Gin Masters
Gold Medal — The Spirits Business 2021
Silver Medal — San Francisco World Spirits Competition 2021 & 2020
Certified Craft Distilled Spirit — American Distilling Institute
🪵 Wood & Finish: First-fill Croatian Quercus robur + American Quercus alba · Batch 2/2023
View on oldpilots.com →
ABV 40% · 700ml · Wheat Distillate

Vodka

Pure bliss, bottled. Crafted from the finest wheat distillate in a cutting-edge iStill column still, purified four times through specialised filters including platinum. A masterpiece of crystal clarity and unmatched smoothness.

Each bottle is hand-filled, stamped and sealed with premium wax and a satin tear strip — featuring a pilot's map on the back and sleek aluminium labels. Velvety texture with a subtly oily body; aromatically neutral with faint hints of star anise and cotton. Perfect for mixology, revamping classic cocktails and new creations alike.

Awards
Gold Medal — World Vodka Awards 2023 (Best Croatian Varietal Vodka)
Gold Medal — World Vodka Awards 2023 Design (World's Best Design Vodka)
Gold Medal — World Vodka Awards 2022 (Best Croatian Pure Neutral Vodka)
Gold Medal — San Francisco World Spirits Competition 2022
Silver Medal — IWSC 2022 (90 points)
🌾 Base: Premium wheat distillate · Filtered 4× through platinum · iStill column still
View on oldpilots.com →
ABV 43% · 700ml · Limited Edition · 1,800 Bottles

Strawberry Gin

A collector's gem and a seasonal delight. Limited to just 1,800 handcrafted bottles, the Strawberry Gin delivers the taste of early summer — fresh, vibrant and entirely natural.

Crafted with locally sourced prime Croatian strawberries, harmoniously blending fruity notes with botanical accents. Free from artificial flavours, colouring or preservatives — gluten-free, vegan, allergen-free and non-GMO. Its unique labelling and elegant design make it a prized addition to any collection and a natural gifting piece. Perfect for summertime cocktails or a refreshing G&T.

Awards
Gold Medal — Las Vegas Global Spirits Awards
93 Points — International Drink Specialists: "Fresh natural strawberries in a crisp classic dry gin"
🍓 1,800 bottles · Locally sourced Croatian strawberries · No artificial ingredients
View on oldpilots.com →
Limited Edition · Collector's Piece · Hand-numbered

Art Edition Gin 2025 —
Brane Doričić

Where craft distilling meets the bold language of tattoo art. A meeting of two worlds — created in collaboration with renowned Rijeka-based tattooer Brane Doričić.

The label features a striking WWII pin-up girl illustration — the kind fighter pilots painted on the tails of their aircraft — executed in Brane's signature traditional tattoo style. Each bottle hand-numbered and limited. At every event where Old Pilot's has been presented, the Art Edition has been described by buyers as "eye candy" — it stops the conversation and tells the story instantly. An exceptional gifting piece and bar display item.

UK Opportunity

Ideal for gifting accounts, private members clubs, press seeding, and the Art Edition channel at Taste of Croatia – Borough Market. High average transaction value with a story that sells itself.

View on duhuboci.com →

Gin & Tonic / Vodka & Soda Cans

Perfect mix of flavour and convenience, ready to enjoy anytime, anywhere. Supplied directly from the distillery in Croatia. Already confirmed for the CNTO Peka Party on 9 June 2026.

Opens a separate distribution channel — convenience retail, travel retail, sporting events and on-trade venues seeking premium RTDs. 67% of UK spirits drinkers are open to premium pre-mixed cocktails in bars.

Market Intelligence

UK Gin & Hospitality
Market 2026

Understanding the landscape Old Pilot's is entering is critical to positioning the brand correctly and prioritising the right channels. The UK spirits market is in premiumisation mode — a structural tailwind that directly favours a brand like Old Pilot's.

UK Spirits Market Value 2026
£25.7B

Growing at 3.18% CAGR to 2034. Ultra-premium segment growing fastest at 8.5% CAGR — the exact tier Old Pilot's occupies.

UK Premium Spirits Market
10.1%

CAGR 2026–2033 for premium spirits. The segment is expected to more than double in value by 2033 — from £13.8B to £29.4B.

Cocktail Bars — UK Growth
+17.4%

Cocktail bar numbers grew 17.4% in 2024 alone. More premium on-trade venues means more listing opportunities for premium craft gins.

Premium Gin Sales Growth
+20%

Premium and craft gin sales increased approximately 20% in the past year. Consumers are choosing fewer, better drinks — quality over quantity.

On-Trade Bartender Effect
Direct

Brands discovered in on-trade cocktail bars directly drive off-trade purchase intent. Bartender advocacy is the most efficient acquisition channel for premium craft spirits.

RTD Market
+Fast

RTD gin grew rapidly in 2025. 67% of category drinkers are open to premium pre-mixed cocktails in bars. Old Pilot's RTD range is perfectly timed.

Key Market Trends — What They Mean for Old Pilot's

Premiumisation is structural, not cyclical

UK consumers are drinking less but spending more per occasion. The market is explicitly moving away from standard spirits toward premium, craft and provenance-led products. Old Pilot's sits in exactly the right tier.

Old Pilot's Position

Award-winning Croatian craft gin with a fighter pilot founder story and hand-picked Mediterranean botanicals. This is exactly what premium buyers are looking for — authenticity, provenance, craft.

Craft and unique provenance drive discovery

Brands with unique stories, high-quality ingredients and distinctive flavours are seeing the strongest growth. The "where is it from?" question is now a positive buying signal — not a barrier. Croatian craft is genuinely rare and memorable.

Old Pilot's Position

Croatia is a destination the UK travel market actively promotes. The CNTO relationship, British-Croatian Business Club, and travel trade network are unique marketing assets most UK spirits brands simply cannot access.

On-trade remains the brand-building engine

The commercial channel — bars, hotels, members clubs — is where premium brands are discovered. Leading spirits brands invest heavily in bartender training, menu placements and ambassador networks because on-trade visibility directly converts to off-trade sales and repeat purchases.

Old Pilot's Position

20+ warm on-trade pipeline venues are waiting to be converted. Two active reorders at premium London venues confirm the product is already doing its job — the only missing piece is activation assets.

Market headwinds are real but targeted

The UK spirits market experienced volume pressure in 2025 due to the income squeeze and cost-of-living challenge. Festival and events revenues fell sharply year-on-year. However, premium segments have shown resilience — and the income squeeze actually accelerates premiumisation as people trade up on fewer occasions.

Strategic Response

Focus on high-value on-trade listings (premium hotels, private clubs) rather than volume events. Each listing here delivers brand equity and margin — not just revenue. The CNTO network gives access to an audience that is not income-squeezed.

Navy Strength and Barrel Aged are the fastest-growing sub-categories

Navy Strength Gin is the fastest-growing gin product type globally (8.4% CAGR 2026–2034), driven by bartender interest in high-ABV expressions for craft cocktails. Barrel-aged gins are similarly gaining traction as bartenders seek depth and complexity.

Old Pilot's Position

Old Pilot's Barrel Aged Gin — aged in American and Croatian oak — sits directly in the fastest-growing premium sub-category. This should be the lead product for bartender-facing sampling and menu development conversations in 2026.

Where Old Pilot's Fits the UK Market Map

Channel Priority 1

Premium On-Trade

Private members clubs, luxury hotel bars, craft cocktail bars. High margin, high influence. Where premium brands are discovered and reputations are built. Old Pilot's already has 20+ warm venues in this tier.

Channel Priority 2

Curated Retail & Gifting

Taste of Croatia at Borough Market, specialist retailers, gifting platforms. The Art Edition and gift set are purpose-built for this channel. High average transaction value, strong brand storytelling environment.

Channel Priority 3

Cultural & Trade Events

CNTO events, Imbibe Live, WTM. Old Pilot's has a unique access advantage through the Croatian cultural and travel trade network. This channel builds relationships and earned media — not just sales.

Confirmed Stocked Venues

Where Old Pilot's Is Live Today

13 confirmed accounts across premium hotels, private members clubs and retail. Two accounts — Home House London and Amici Soho — have already reordered, confirming strong product-market fit where activation has occurred.

Key Relationship — 7 Hospitality Group

The majority of confirmed hotel accounts are managed through a single group relationship at 7 Hospitality. Brian McNair (brian.mcnair@7hospitality.co.uk) is the central commercial contact covering properties across Scotland and England — a single relationship unlocking multiple venue listings simultaneously.

Buyer Feedback — What We Heard

Across every venue type, buyer and bartender feedback on Old Pilot's has been consistent: the product is exceptional. It has been repeatedly described as one of the best gins tasted by buyers at the UK's most prestigious venues.

Soho House approved Old Pilot's at venue level and submitted for national group review — a significant endorsement given the scale of their global portfolio.

"One of the best gins we've tasted — the aviation story really lands with our guests."
"The Croatian heritage and craft credentials are a genuine talking point at the bar."
"We'd love to list it — we just need photography and POS before we can go to menu."
The Key Insight

The primary barrier to listings across all 20+ pipeline venues has been identical: activation assets. Not the product. Not the price. Not the story.

Where activation occurred — Home House, Amici — reorders followed. Commission assets, activate accounts, convert pipeline. The product does the rest.

—Professional photography and cocktail imagery
—POS materials — table cards, menu inserts, branded display
—Signature serve proposals — bespoke per venue
—Staff training and activation event support

From Duh u Boci

NEBO —
Croatian Small Batch Whisky

A genuinely rare proposition in the UK market. The second major brand from Distillery Duh u Boci — and a natural companion to Old Pilot's.

The Brand

What is NEBO?

NEBO (meaning "sky" in Croatian) is a small-batch whisky range from Duh u Boci, available in four distinct expressions. Each reflects a different facet of Croatian craft — from a brewery collaboration to peated single malt.

NEBO shares the same distillery DNA as Old Pilot's — the same aviation backstory, the same Croatian provenance, the same premium craft ethos. UK buyers who already know Old Pilot's have an immediate framework for understanding NEBO. The shared story is a commercial advantage.

Croatian whisky is a genuine first-mover opportunity in the UK — a category the market has never encountered. The novelty is a conversation starter, not a barrier, and it aligns perfectly with the UK's appetite for provenance-led premium spirits.

UK Opportunity

The existing Old Pilot's buyer network — 13 live accounts and 20+ pipeline venues — is the most efficient launchpad for NEBO. Warm introductions through a trusted relationship convert far faster than cold outreach, and the origin story connects the two brands seamlessly.

NEBO Range — Four Expressions
Four Grain Whisky — Flight 1
Corn, barley, spelt and rye. Combines Scottish precision with American boldness. Aged 2 years in 30-litre virgin American oak (Char #3), then finished in 190-litre ex-rye barrels. Notes of ripe apple, peach, gingerbread, vanilla and white chocolate. Clean, elegant finish.
Flight 1 — Available Now · duhuboci.com
Peated Single Malt — Flight 2
Scottish high-quality barley. Three malts: peat-smoked (30–45 ppm phenols), classic two-row base, Belgian specialty. Aged in new Croatian Quercus robur, ex-peated-bourbon American oak, finished in 30-year Islay barrels. Notes of smoky ash, lemon, roasted coffee, dark chocolate. Long warm finish.
Flight 2 — Available Now · duhuboci.com
Single Malt — Flight 3
Three two-row base malts with beechwood-smoked (not peated) malt for a milder, warmer smokiness. Aged 48 months in 190-litre ex-bourbon American oak plus 30-litre Croatian oak casks. Notes of dried grains, apple, pear, dried plum, caramel, cloves and vanilla. Medium-length polished finish.
Flight 3 — Available Now · duhuboci.com
Medvedgrad Collaboration — Flight 4
Duh u Boci × Medvedgrad Brewery. Two Croatian craft pioneers — brewing and distillation combined. Character begins in the brewhouse and matures in the cask. A unique collaboration expression with rich malt character and craft provenance story.
Flight 4 — Available Now · duhuboci.com
UK Launch Strategy

How We Launch NEBO

1
Q2 / Q3 2026 — Foundation

Secure & Seed

  • Secure bonded stock (~10 cases per SKU) via LCB — bonded route preferred
  • Commission core assets: brand story, photography, trade sheet, serve suggestions
  • Introduce NEBO to existing Old Pilot's buyer network — warm contacts first
  • Soft introduction at CNTO Peka Party (June 9) if stock permits
  • Croatian whisky in a Croatian cultural setting — the story writes itself
2
Q3 2026 — Trade Activation

Enter the Trade

  • Target private clubs and bars already engaged on Old Pilot's — cross-brand listings are efficient
  • Imbibe Live (July) — ideal trade debut for NEBO alongside Old Pilot's
  • Approach Croatia Airlines and British Croatian Business Club as ambassadors
  • Launch bartender sampling programme — same model that drove Old Pilot's reorders
  • Signature serve development for whisky-focused accounts
3
Q4 2026 — Scale

Retail & E-commerce

  • Introduce to Majestic Wine HQ — contact established via Old Pilot's
  • Explore gift bundling: Old Pilot's + NEBO Croatian spirits gift set
  • Launch via Inter-Send e-commerce alongside Old Pilot's
  • World Travel Market (November) — Croatian tourism angle is powerful here
  • CNTO September event — second window to convert June relationships
Strategic Recommendations

Recommendations from Ajay Kawa

Having managed UK operations since the brand's entry in 2024 — covering logistics, sales, hospitality engagement, duty management and market development — the following recommendations are offered to support Old Pilot's and Duh u Boci as the brand enters its next phase in the UK.

Context

Year one delivered strong qualitative traction: the product is respected, the brand story resonates, and the pipeline is warm. The core learning is simple — activation unlocks everything. Where activation happened, reorders followed. The recommendations below are built around that insight.

01
Priority: Immediate

Commission the Activation Asset Suite — This Is the Critical Unlock

Every single pipeline venue that has not yet converted has cited the same blocker: the absence of professional photography, cocktail imagery, and POS materials. This is not a sales problem — it is an asset problem. Invest in a single professional shoot covering the full product range: hero bottle shots, cocktail serves for each SKU, lifestyle/bar imagery, and branded display materials. This one investment unblocks 20+ venues simultaneously.

Venues like Rosewood, The Gibson, Mr Fogg's, Annabel's and 5 Hertford Street are ready to list — they have said so. They are waiting for assets, not further persuasion.

02
Priority: Immediate

Invest in the CNTO Relationship as a Long-Term Marketing Asset

The partnership with the Croatian National Tourist Office is a competitive advantage that most UK spirits brands simply cannot replicate. The CNTO events bring together travel media, trade buyers, cultural influencers and hospitality professionals in a single room — an audience that is warm to Croatia, warm to premium products, and influential across multiple channels. The June 9 Peka Party is the immediate opportunity. The September event is the follow-up window. Both should be treated as flagship brand moments, not peripheral activities.

The previous CNTO event produced 11 journalist contacts and 85 travel trade contacts — all warm. That network is a marketing asset with a very long runway.

03
Priority: Immediate

Lead with the Barrel Aged Gin in All Bartender Conversations

Barrel-aged gin is the fastest-growing premium gin sub-category globally. Bartenders at the venues in the pipeline — The Gibson, Rosewood, Dukes — are precisely the audience most excited by complex, barrel-influenced spirits. The Old Pilot's Barrel Aged Gin (American and Croatian oak, 6 months, 330 bottles per batch) is a compelling trade proposition that most competitors cannot match for story and provenance. It should be the flagship product in all bartender-facing sampling and menu development conversations.

The LDG and Vodka will follow naturally once bartenders are advocates — but the Barrel Aged Gin is the product most likely to generate bartender enthusiasm and word-of-mouth in the trade.

04
Priority: Q3 2026

Formalise the 7 Hospitality Group Relationship and Expand It

The 7 Hospitality Group relationship — managed through Brian McNair — has delivered 11 live hotel accounts from a single contact. This is an exceptionally efficient account structure and should be formalised with a supplier agreement, annual volume commitment, staff training programme, and co-branded activation events. The relationship should also be leveraged to identify other hotel groups of similar structure — one buyer, multiple properties — that could replicate this model across the UK.

Hotel group accounts offer volume stability, brand consistency, and the kind of premium environment that reinforces Old Pilot's positioning. They are more reliable than individual venue accounts in a volatile market.

05
Priority: Q3 2026

Review UK Pricing — Particularly London Dry Gin and Vodka

The first-year margin analysis shows that LDG and Vodka are generating 4% and 3% gross margins respectively — reflecting the full duty and logistics burden of a new market entry at the initial import pricing. As volume grows and the distribution model stabilises, these margins should be reviewed and pricing adjusted to reflect the true UK landed cost while maintaining premium positioning. The Barrel Aged Gin at 12% margin provides a clear model for where the full range should be heading.

Sustainable margin is a prerequisite for the brand investing in UK activation. Pricing must be right before volume commitments are made at scale.

06
Priority: Q3 2026

Launch NEBO Through the Existing Old Pilot's Network — Not Separately

NEBO's most efficient route to market is through the trust and relationships already built for Old Pilot's. Buyers who have already listed or sampled Old Pilot's have a warm disposition toward Duh u Boci as a producer. Introducing NEBO as the second brand from the same distillery — with the same aviation heritage — is a natural conversation, not a new pitch. The two brands should be sold as a portfolio from the outset, not as independent launches.

Croatian whisky is a first-mover opportunity in the UK — but first-mover advantage requires speed. Using the existing network is the fastest route to early listings and bartender advocacy.

07
Priority: Q3 2026

Activate Taste of Croatia at Borough Market as a Flagship Retail Moment

Borough Market is one of the highest-footfall food and drink destinations in London, with a customer base that is precisely Old Pilot's target demographic — food-curious, premium-minded, internationally aware, and open to discovery. Taste of Croatia offers a curated Croatian retail environment where Old Pilot's story makes natural sense. The gifting set and Art Edition are purpose-built for this environment. This account should be treated as a flagship retail activation with regular sampling, seasonal promotions and staff training.

A visible, well-activated presence at Borough Market provides direct consumer sales, tourist reach, media discovery opportunities and organic social content — all in a single location.

08
Priority: Q4 2026

Launch E-commerce via Inter-Send for Next-Day UK Delivery

The Inter-Send fulfilment trial was prepared but not yet activated. Launching a UK e-commerce operation — with next-day delivery, cocktail kits, and the gift set — completes the consumer purchase journey that on-trade discovery begins. When a consumer tries Old Pilot's at Home House or The Ned and wants to buy it for home, there needs to be a frictionless, branded UK purchase channel ready. This also supports the RTD range, subscription models, and seasonal gifting campaigns.

E-commerce is not a primary revenue driver in year one — but it is a critical infrastructure investment that enables everything else to compound: on-trade trial converts to repeat home purchase, and media coverage drives search and direct sales.

09
Priority: Ongoing

Commit to Monthly Reporting and Quarterly Strategic Reviews

A consistent reporting rhythm between Duh u Boci and the UK operation is essential for the brand to make good decisions at pace. Monthly reports should cover: account activity, stock levels, pipeline movements, event outcomes, and market intelligence. Quarterly reviews should assess KPIs, pricing, channel performance, and strategic priorities. This discipline — which was not formally in place in year one — will ensure Croatia is always informed and able to respond to market signals quickly.

The UK spirits market moves fast. Buyer preferences, menu cycles, and event windows all have short lead times. A well-informed principal is a better partner and a more decisive investor in UK marketing and growth.

2026 Marketing & Action Plan

The Path Forward

A structured quarter-by-quarter plan for UK market development in 2026 — covering trade activation, media outreach, events, NEBO introduction and e-commerce launch. The plan is activation-first and builds sequentially from the warm relationships already established.

Guiding Principle

The product is exceptional. The pipeline is warm. The story is unique. The 2026 plan is not about starting from scratch — it is about converting what is already built through consistent activation, asset investment, and strategic use of the CNTO cultural network that no other UK gin brand has access to.

Q2 2026 — June

Launch & Establish

  • Confirm activation stand at CNTO Peka Party — June 9, London. Display full OP bottling range.
  • Pre-event media outreach to Mary Novakovich, Rudolf Abraham, Tristan Rutherford — UK's top Croatia media specialists
  • Introduce new UK distribution arrangements to existing accounts (7 Hospitality, Home House, Amici)
  • Commission professional photography shoot — full product range, cocktail serves, lifestyle bar imagery
  • Confirm NEBO bonded stock requirements and initiate LCB transfer process
  • Soft-introduce NEBO to existing accounts as "second brand from same distillery"
Q3 2026 — July / August / September

Activate & Convert

  • Imbibe Live (July) — attend as Old Pilot's + NEBO. First major trade debut for NEBO.
  • Re-engage top-5 pipeline venues with assets in hand: Annabel's, The Gibson, Mr Fogg's, Rosewood, Boisdale
  • Launch Barrel Aged Gin bartender sampling programme across London cocktail bars
  • Develop signature serves for key pipeline venues — bespoke per account
  • CNTO September event — second activation window. Convert June relationships to listings.
  • Submit NEBO to key buyers: Majestic Wine HQ, private members clubs, Inception Group
  • Activate Taste of Croatia Borough Market with regular sampling and gifting set display
Q4 2026 — October / November / December

Scale & Retail Push

  • The Restaurant Show (Autumn) — target Inception Group, Martello Hall, Mr Porter pipeline
  • World Travel Market (November) — Old Pilot's + NEBO. Croatian tourism angle. Engage WTM contacts already warm from CNTO network.
  • Launch Inter-Send e-commerce for next-day UK delivery — Old Pilot's full range + NEBO
  • Christmas gifting campaign — Art Edition and gift set, targeted at corporates and premium retail
  • Approach Majestic Wine nationally once fulfilment infrastructure is confirmed
  • Year-end review and 2027 planning with Duh u Boci — KPIs, pricing, channel assessment
Q1 2027 — January / February / March

Build on Momentum

  • HRC trade show — consolidate hotel group relationships. Target new hotel groups with 7H model.
  • Q1 menu cycle — key listing window for London on-trade. Follow up all sampled venues with assets and proposals.
  • Launch London Wine Fair (May) presence — broader premium spirits buyer audience
  • Assess NEBO listings and adjust activation approach based on Q3/Q4 feedback
  • Expand regional coverage — Manchester, Birmingham and Bristol on-trade development
  • Explore Croatian Airlines partnership for travel retail and in-flight gifting proposition

Where to Be Seen

UK Drinks & Trade
Events Calendar 2026

The UK drinks calendar is one of the busiest in the world. These are the events where listings are won, bartenders are converted, media coverage is earned, and distributor relationships are built. For a brand at Old Pilot's stage, showing up consistently at the right events is as important as the product itself.

Priority Approach

Not every event requires a stand — and at this stage, budget should be deployed strategically. Some events are best attended as a visitor for networking and intelligence-gathering. Others — particularly BCB London and WTM — warrant a presence. The CNTO events are the highest-value opportunities because they are exclusive to the Croatian cultural network that no other UK gin brand can access.

Key Drinks Dates — UK Calendar 2026

Beyond trade shows, the UK drinks calendar has globally recognised moments that brands can leverage for social media, press outreach, venue activations and PR. These dates are used by every major spirits brand to time product pushes, menu launches and media pitches.

March
St Patrick's Day — 17 Mar

International spirits moment. Irish whiskey focus but all premium spirits benefit from the on-trade energy. Good for Irish and European craft positioning.

May
World Cocktail Day — 13 May

Major global moment for cocktail culture. Signature serve launches, social content push, press pitches. Perfect for Old Pilot's bartender partnerships.

June
World Gin Day — 1st Sat Jun

The UK's biggest gin celebration. Global reach, massive social amplification, on-trade activations everywhere. Old Pilot's should own this day on social and at key venues.

June–August
London Cocktail Season

Peak G&T and cocktail consumption period. Menu launches, terrace activations, summer serves. Key window for converting pipeline accounts with signature serve proposals.

May
World Whisky Day — 3rd Sat May

A major moment for NEBO. Croatian whisky on World Whisky Day with a compelling first-mover story. High media interest potential — pitch to Mary Novakovich, Tristan Rutherford and Rudolf Abraham.

November–December
Christmas Gifting Season

Highest-value retail period. The Art Edition and gift set are purpose-built for this window. Target Borough Market, premium retailers, corporate gifting and press gifting lists in October for December placement.

Digital Presence

Social Media Strategy
for the UK Market

Premium craft spirits brands live or die on social media in the UK market. Bartenders, buyers, journalists and consumers all research brands on Instagram and LinkedIn before committing to a listing or a purchase. Old Pilot's has an extraordinary story to tell — the question is how to tell it consistently, visually, and in a way that builds trade advocacy and consumer desire simultaneously.

Instagram
Primary Channel
Premium spirits discovery & bartender advocacy
LinkedIn
Trade Channel
B2B, buyer relationships & press
TikTok
Growth Channel
Consumer awareness & cocktail culture
PR
Earned Media
Journalists, food & drinks media

Channel Strategy — Instagram

Instagram is the non-negotiable channel for premium spirits in the UK. It is where bartenders discover brands, where buyers research listings, and where consumers build desire. The aesthetic should match the website: dark, editorial, product-first — with the aviation story woven through every caption.

Content Pillar 1

The Product & The Craft

Close-up bottle photography, distillery imagery, Croatian botanicals, the barrel room. Premium, dark, editorial. Every shot should look like it belongs on a restaurant menu.

Post frequency: 3× per week. Hero content.
Content Pillar 2

The Serves & Cocktails

Signature serve recipes, bartender hands, garnish close-ups, at-home cocktail content. Tag the venues and bartenders. This content drives the most saves and shares from both trade and consumers.

Post frequency: 2× per week. Reels perform best.
Content Pillar 3

The Story & Heritage

The founders' pilot background, Croatia, the distillery, botanical sourcing, behind-the-scenes. This is the content that builds brand loyalty and differentiates Old Pilot's from every other gin in the market.

Post frequency: 1–2× per week. Stories + Feed.
Content Pillar 4

Venue & Trade Features

When Old Pilot's lands on a menu or bar shelf, post it and tag the venue. Repost bartender content. Feature team members at accounts. This content builds social proof and drives other buyers to reach out.

Post every new listing. Always tag the venue.
Content Pillar 5

Events & Activations

Live coverage of CNTO events, trade shows, tastings and pop-ups. Behind-the-scenes Stories. Reels from the events. This content builds brand credibility and shows buyers and media that Old Pilot's is active and present in the market.

Real-time Stories + post-event Reels.
Content Pillar 6

Croatia & Culture

Lean into the Croatian identity — landscape, food culture, coast imagery paired with Old Pilot's. This positions the brand within a lifestyle aspiration, not just a category. The CNTO partnership makes this authentic and shareable.

1× per week. High engagement content.

Post Ideas — Ready to Use

World Gin Day
1st Sat, June
Instagram + TikTok
Caption idea:

"They said you couldn't make a world-class gin in Croatia. Two ex-pilots disagreed. Happy #WorldGinDay — pour yourself a little slice of the sky. 🍸✈️ #OldPilotsGin #MadeInTheSkies #CroatianGin #CraftSpirits #PremiumGin"

New Listing
When a venue lists
Instagram Stories + Feed
Caption idea:

"Now flying at 30,000 feet above [Venue Name]. Old Pilot's has landed on the menu — ask your bartender for the signature serve. 📍 @[venuetag] #OldPilotsGin #MadeInTheSkies #[VenueCity]"

Barrel Aged
Product hero
Instagram Reel
Caption idea:

"Six months in Croatian and American oak. One bottle of something special. Our Barrel Aged Gin — the gin that thinks it's a whisky. 330 bottles per batch. Each one numbered. ✦ #BarrelAgedGin #OldPilotsGin #SmallBatch #CraftSpirits #CroatianGin"

NEBO Teaser
Pre-launch
Instagram + LinkedIn
Caption idea:

"Something new from the skies. Croatia's first small-batch whisky is coming to the UK. NEBO. Meaning sky. Made by the same hands that built Old Pilot's. More soon. 🥃 #NEBO #CroatianWhisky #SmallBatch #ComingSoon #DuhUBoci"

Art Edition
Gifting & Christmas
Instagram Feed
Caption idea:

"Every WWII pilot's tradition was to paint something extraordinary on the tail of their aircraft. We painted ours on a bottle. Art Edition 2025 — collaboration with @[tattooer]. Limited. Numbered. Collectable. ✈️ Link in bio. #ArtEdition #OldPilotsGin #LimitedEdition #GiftIdeas #CraftGin"

LinkedIn — Trade & B2B Strategy

LinkedIn is where UK on-trade buyers, procurement managers, F&B directors, and hospitality group heads are most reachable. This is the channel for trade announcements, venue partnership news, and positioning the brand as a serious UK market player.

→Announce every new UK listing with a professional post tagging the venue
→Post trade event attendance (BCB, WTM, CNTO) before and after to build credibility
→Share UK market milestones: first reorder, new hotel group partnership, award wins
→Use LinkedIn outreach to connect with F&B directors and purchasing managers at target pipeline venues before calling
→NEBO announcement as a LinkedIn-first reveal — trade audience most relevant for a new whisky brand

Hashtag Strategy & Key Tags

Consistent hashtag use builds discoverability and ensures the brand appears in searches used by UK buyers, bartenders and journalists. Use a layered approach: brand-owned + category + location.

#OldPilotsGin #MadeInTheSkies #NEBO #CroatianGin #CraftGin #PremiumSpirits #CraftSpirits #LondonBars #GinLovers #UKSpirits #BarrelAgedGin #SmallBatch #Croatia #CroatianWhisky #DuhUBoci #WorldGinDay #GinAndTonic #CocktailCulture
Bartender Advocacy Tip

When a bartender creates a serve using Old Pilot's, repost it immediately and tag them. Bartenders have deeply engaged trade audiences. One repost from a respected London bartender reaches hundreds of other bartenders and buyers. This is worth more than paid advertising at this stage.

The Journey Ahead

2026–2027
Growth Roadmap

A strategic roadmap for Old Pilot's and NEBO across the next 18 months — from consolidating the UK foundation built in 2025, through the activation push of 2026, to establishing a scalable and profitable UK operation by end of 2027.

The Vision

By the end of 2027, Old Pilot's should be a recognised premium gin brand stocked across 40+ UK on-trade accounts, with active reorders, a functioning e-commerce channel, confirmed retail distribution, and NEBO establishing its own presence as the UK's first Croatian whisky. The Croatian cultural identity — through the CNTO, Croatian Airlines, and Borough Market — should be a recognised and differentiating brand asset.

The Single Most Important Thing

Everything in this roadmap flows from one decision: invest in activation assets. Professional photography, POS materials, and signature serve proposals unlock the 20+ venues already waiting. That one investment — made in June/July 2026 — is the lever that moves everything else. Without it, the pipeline stays warm but unconverted. With it, 2026 becomes the year Old Pilot's goes from market entrant to established UK premium brand.

Practical Guide

UK Duty, Compliance
& Distributor Checklist

The UK alcohol market is one of the most heavily regulated in the world. Understanding duty, bonding, and compliance requirements before entering the market protects cash flow, avoids penalties, and ensures the brand is positioned with credible, fully licensed trade partners. This section explains exactly what is required — and provides a practical checklist for any new distributor or sales agent.

Why This Matters for Duh u Boci

The previous UK operation encountered a real-world duty challenge: stock imported without clarity on whether duty was pre-paid or bonded. This cost time and created cashflow pressure. The guidance below ensures that any future import, distributor engagement, or stock transfer is handled cleanly from the outset.

UK Alcohol Duty — How It Works

Since August 2023, UK alcohol duty is calculated on a pure ABV basis — the stronger the product, the higher the duty. This replaced the old flat-rate-per-category system. Rates increased again from 1 February 2026 in line with RPI inflation.

Spirits (Gin, Vodka, Whisky) above 22% ABV
£32.79

Per litre of pure alcohol (LPA) — effective from 1 Feb 2026. This is the rate applicable to all Old Pilot's and NEBO products.

Duty on a 70cl bottle at 45% ABV (e.g. Old Pilot's LDG)
~£10.31

Calculation: 0.7L × 0.45 ABV × £32.79 = £10.32 duty per bottle. Plus VAT at 20% on top of the duty-inclusive price.

VAT Rate
20%

Standard VAT applies to all alcoholic products. Applied on the duty-inclusive retail price. VAT can be reclaimed on stock purchased for resale.

How to Calculate Duty — Step by Step

1
Find the LPA
Litres of Pure Alcohol = Volume (L) × ABV ÷ 100

e.g. 0.70L × 45 ÷ 100 = 0.315 LPA
2
Apply the Rate
LPA × £32.79 (2026 rate)

0.315 × £32.79 = £10.33 duty per bottle
3
Add to Cost Price
Supplier cost + duty + freight + insurance = true landed cost

Landed cost drives your minimum viable selling price
4
Add VAT at 20%
VAT is applied on the full duty-inclusive selling price when sold to consumers. VAT-registered businesses can reclaim on stock for resale.
Key Practical Point — Bonded vs Duty Paid

Bonded (duty suspended): Stock is held in an HMRC-approved bonded warehouse. Duty is not paid until bottles are released from bond into the UK market. This is the preferred route — it preserves cash flow and gives the importer full control over when duty is triggered.

Duty paid: Duty is paid at the point of import or release. Once paid, it cannot be reclaimed if stock is unsold or returned. This creates cashflow risk, particularly on larger shipments.

Recommendation: Always import into bond. Release only what has been sold or is confirmed for immediate sale. This is how professional UK spirits importers manage all stock.

Bonded Warehousing — How It Works in Practice

A bonded warehouse (also called an excise warehouse) is an HMRC-approved secure facility where alcohol can be stored duty-suspended. The warehouse keeper is licensed by HMRC and takes responsibility for the goods while in bond.

1
Goods arrive in the UK — enter bond immediately
Stock is imported under customs procedure and moved directly to the bonded warehouse without duty being triggered. A customs agent handles the import declaration.
2
Stock is stored duty-suspended
The warehouse issues a warehouse receipt. Stock can be held indefinitely in bond, transferred to other bonded facilities (e.g. another distributor's bond), or exported duty-free to other countries.
3
Duty is paid when stock is released from bond
When an order is confirmed, the relevant number of cases is released from bond. Duty is calculated at that point and paid to HMRC. The AWRS-registered distributor handles this process.
4
Duty cost is built into the sale price
The distributor's selling price to the trade must recover the duty cost, freight, warehousing fees and margin. This is why landed cost analysis is essential before agreeing UK trade pricing.

Required UK Registrations & Licences

Any business importing, storing or selling alcohol in the UK must hold specific HMRC registrations. These are non-negotiable — trading without them is a criminal offence and can result in stock seizure, fines and prosecution.

RegistrationWhat It IsWho Needs ItHow to Get It
AWRS — Alcohol Wholesaler Registration Scheme HMRC approval to sell alcohol to other businesses in the UK. Issues a Unique Reference Number (URN) which must appear on all invoices. Any business selling alcohol to other UK businesses for resale — importers, distributors, agents Apply via HMRC online. Requires business plan, supplier/customer details, due diligence procedures, storage confirmation. Processing takes 45 days.
WOWGR — Warehousekeepers and Owners of Warehoused Goods Regulations HMRC approval to receive, hold or move duty-suspended goods. Required to operate a bonded warehouse or to be the owner of goods in bond. Anyone who owns goods held in a bonded warehouse duty-suspended, or operates such a warehouse Apply via HMRC. Can use a third-party approved warehouse keeper (e.g. LCB, Grayson's) — they hold the WOWGR, you as owner must be registered as an Owners of Warehoused Goods holder.
UK Customs EORI Number Economic Operators Registration and Identification number — required for all imports into the UK Any business importing goods into the UK from outside the UK (including from EU) Register online with HMRC. Usually issued within 3 working days. Required before the first shipment.
UK VAT Registration Required if UK taxable turnover exceeds £90,000 or if the business chooses to register voluntarily UK-based distributors and importers who are VAT registered can reclaim VAT on stock purchases Register via HMRC online. VAT number must appear on all UK invoices.
Premises Licence / Personal Licence Required to sell alcohol directly to consumers from a fixed premises or at events Retail venues, event organisers, tasting rooms — not required for trade sales (B2B) Apply to the local licensing authority. Personal licence requires a DPS (Designated Premises Supervisor).

New Distributor Checklist — What to Verify Before Signing

Before appointing any UK distributor or agent, Duh u Boci should verify the following. A distributor who cannot confirm these points should not be handling Old Pilot's or NEBO in the UK market.

Legal & Compliance
Logistics & Storage
Commercial & Financial
Sales & Marketing

New UK Sales Person — Day One Checklist

Any sales representative or brand ambassador covering Old Pilot's or NEBO in the UK should complete the following before their first customer meeting.

Know the Product
Know the Market
Ready to Sell
Useful HMRC Links

Check AWRS registration: gov.uk/check-alcohol-wholesaler-registration
Apply for AWRS: gov.uk/guidance/alcohol-wholesaler-registration-scheme
UK duty rates: gov.uk/government/publications/alcohol-duty-rates-change
WOWGR registration: gov.uk/guidance/register-as-a-warehousekeeper-or-owner-of-warehoused-goods
EORI registration: gov.uk/eori
